threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you get off at Dalston Junction. Major rookie mistake, mate.
Caught a whiff of jerk chicken on the N37 last night—nothing like South London fare at 2 AM. Some said it was an omen for the Tube strikes!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was living here? Watching a group of kids turn a broken bus stop into a makeshift skate park. Pure Neukölln creativity.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, some cafes serve leftovers as 'pay what you can' meals—definitely a Berlin mix of generosity and practicality!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals trust the taco truck on 24th over the fancy spots. You can spot them by how quickly they grab a horchata on the way.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watching the Muni driver stop mid-route to help a lost tourist was classic Tenderloin—where kindness peeks through the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and fancy cafés, but the real vibe is in the microbreweries and late-night taverna chats.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitance to order anything but a café con leche—it’s like they think it’s the only drink here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still using the Tube to get around; real locals know the 30-minute bus ride is part of the experience and way quicker.
Last night on the 45, saw a bloke serenading the driver with an old school reggae track. Only in Brixton, right? Had everyone laughing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found myself at the corner of Hermannstraße, listening to an impromptu concert from the kebab shop across the street. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar where they serve cocktails in jars, while a DJ spins classic hits from the '90s, all amid murals of quirky street art.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re a local when they can name the taco truck by its sauce color and make eye contact while dodging a pigeon.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 Fulton stuck behind a food truck delivering hot empanadas. Smell was great, but made us all late—classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all tapas are good, but the frozen calamari at that popular place on Verdi is a culinary crime. Locals know to avoid it.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still think they can get a seat on the metro during rush hour. Good luck with that!
